definition: The overall experience a person has when interacting with a product, system, or service, encompassing usability, accessibility, and satisfaction.
extendedDefinition: UX design focuses on understanding user needs, behaviors, and pain points through research and testing to create intuitive and enjoyable interactions. It spans multiple disciplines including information architecture, interaction design, visual design, and content strategy, all working together to reduce friction and meet user goals. A strong UX drives higher adoption rates, customer loyalty, and revenue, while poor UX leads to frustration and churn. Companies that invest in UX consistently outperform competitors, as user-centered products require less support and generate stronger word-of-mouth growth.
examples: Intuitive navigation that reduces the time to complete tasks
Consistent design patterns that build user confidence and predictability
Accessible interfaces that work for users with disabilities
description: User Experience (UX) encompasses all aspects of a user's interaction with a product. Learn how UX design principles improve software usability and adoption.
